title: Billbergia rufa
taxonomy: Billbergia rufa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. It belongs to the genus Billbergia, which is native to South America. This epiphytic species is often found in tropical and subtropical regions, growing on trees and rocks. The Billbergia rufa is characterized by its vibrant red flowers and distinctive rosette-shaped leaves. It is a popular ornamental plant due to its unique appearance and low-maintenance requirements.

intro: Billbergia rufa, commonly known as the Red Billbergia or Red Canna Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its vibrant red, orange, or yellow flowers and waxy, sword-shaped leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Billbergia rufa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Billbergia rufa is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, colds, and coughs, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
